label: Mixpanel
description: Query Mixpanel event data, funnels, retention, and cohorts using a service account.

form.title: Connect Mixpanel
form.subtitle: Authenticate with a Mixpanel service account to access your project's analytics data.

methods.label: Service Account
methods.description: Paste your Mixpanel service account username, secret, and project ID. The recommended approach — no OAuth setup required, works for all Mixpanel plans.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Mixpanel Service Account

Mixpanel service accounts are project-scoped credentials that let Anton query your data without using your personal login.

### Steps

1. Open [Mixpanel](https://mixpanel.com) and navigate to your project.
2. Go to **Project Settings** (gear icon, top-left) → **Service Accounts**.
3. Click **Add Service Account**, give it a name like `Anton`, and click **Create**.
4. Mixpanel displays the **Username** and **Secret** — copy both now. The secret is shown only once.
5. Find your **Project ID**: it appears in the URL (`/project/<id>/`) or under **Project Settings → Overview**.
6. Paste the username, secret, and project ID into the fields below.
7. Select your **Data Residency Region** — choose **EU** if your Mixpanel project is hosted in the EU (eu.mixpanel.com), otherwise leave it as **US**.

### Notes

- The secret is shown only at creation time. If you lose it, delete the service account and create a new one.
- Service accounts have read-only access by default, which is all Anton needs.
- EU-region projects must use the EU endpoint or requests will fail with auth errors.

fields.name: service_account_username
fields.label: Service Account Username
fields.description: Found under Project Settings → Service Accounts after creating the account.

fields.name: service_account_secret
fields.label: Service Account Secret
fields.description: Shown once at creation time. If lost, delete and recreate the service account.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: Project ID
fields.description: Numeric ID found in the project URL or under Project Settings → Overview.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Data Residency Region
fields.description: Select EU if your project is hosted at eu.mixpanel.com, otherwise US.
